headline: Samsung & SK Hynix tumble 9%+ as chip rout spreads
detail: This is the most direct threat to your book. A 9%+ drop in two of the world's biggest chipmakers signals broad sector fear, not just stock-specific noise. SMH, SOXL, and any chip-adjacent names you hold will feel this - and SOXL's 3x leverage means the hit is tripled.
rec: Seriously consider trimming SOXL before the open or setting a mental alert at $210. Your $200 stop is the absolute floor - on margin, waiting for it to hit could mean a much larger realized loss than trimming proactively now.

headline: Apple eyes Chinese-made chips amid foldable push
detail: Apple exploring non-US chip suppliers is a longer-term headwind for US chip names like those inside SMH. It's not an immediate crash catalyst but adds to negative chip sentiment on an already bad day. Doesn't directly hit CRWD or software names.
rec: No action needed today, but this adds another reason not to be adding to SMH or SOXL right now - the sector narrative is turning more negative, not less.

headline: Google loses $4.7B EU antitrust fight
detail: A reminder that big-tech regulatory risk is real and ongoing in Europe. Doesn't hit your specific holdings directly, but broad tech sentiment takes a small hit and QQQ/SPY could see mild pressure. Not a major mover for your book today.
rec: Your QQQ and SPY positions are both small gainers with stops well below current prices - no reason to act on this headline specifically. Hold and monitor.

headline: M&A 'on fire' per Citi UK CEO
detail: Active M&A environment is a background positive for large-cap tech - names like CRWD, PANW, and cybersecurity broadly can be acquisition targets or beneficiaries of consolidation. Doesn't change today's chip-led selloff but is a medium-term positive for your CRWD holding.
rec: No action today - CRWD is your strongest holding (+7.4%) and well above its $590 stop. Just keep holding; this headline supports the longer-term case but doesn't warrant adding on a down day for the sector.
